comment.body:
one more thought for this:
you know, i got and get a lot of job offers from big funds, desks, from the USA to the Middle-East.
i refused all of them, but with some of them i stay in friendship and we talk in private a lot.
very educative.

you know, the shady thing is the size of those condors..
funds like to hide there trades exactly bcs they don't want to influence the market and also they don't want to reveal themselves for their "opponents".
(not counting in the JPM collar bcs thats just a hedging trade)
when you see the condor it is clearly an attempt for local market making.

So this is my very strong and serious impression.

comment.body:
@Alma In your intra-day post, you alluded to net speed profile to be flat to positive, and I looked up dealer positioning before market open, and turning the speed profile concave coming from dealer short gamma below -( making dealer short on RV below the spot at the time)- which would have made left-tail of the implied distribution heavily bought ;
Net short charm means that dealers become shorter delta as time passes, generating a slow buyback flow if we stayed about the downside pivot level - Am I interpreting it correctly
comment.raw_body:
${0} In your intra-day post, you alluded to net speed profile to be flat to positive, and I looked up dealer positioning before market open, and turning the speed profile concave coming from dealer short gamma below -( making dealer short on RV below the spot at the time)- which would have made left-tail of the implied distribution heavily bought ;
Net short charm means that dealers become shorter delta as time passes, generating a slow buyback flow if we stayed about the downside pivot level - Am I interpreting it correctly
